creator: freebsd@bitter-almonds.com
text:
I issued (on FreeBSD 8.4-STABLE r267149 i386; ports version is: r356550) ...

 portmaster  --packages   xine gimp-app
  
... that ended in error I am having since about last month ...

...
===>  Building package for libgsf-1.14.28_1
Creating package /misc/work/ports/misc/ports/devel/libgsf/work/pkg/libgsf-1.14.28_1.tbz
Registering depends: gdk-pixbuf2-2.28.2 jasper-1.900.1_14 tiff-4.0.3_2 freeglut-2.8.1_2 glib-2.36.3_3 gettext-0.18.3.1_1 libGLU-9.0.0 libGL-7.6.1_4 libXdamage-1.1.4_2 libXi-1.7.2_2,1 libXmu-1.1.2_2,1 libXrandr-1.4.2_2 libXxf86vm-1.1.3_2 libXext-1.3.2_2,1 libXfixes-5.0.1_2 libXrender-0.9.8_2 libXt-1.1.4_2,1 libX11-1.6.2_2,1 libxcb-1.10_2 libxml2-2.9.1_1 libiconv-1.14_3 icu-52.1 libffi-3.0.13_1 libdrm-2.4.17_1 libpciaccess-0.13.2_2 libpthread-stubs-0.3_4 pcre-8.34_1 jbigkit-1.6 jpeg-8_5 png-1.5.18 broken_contents perl5.18-5.18.2_3 python27-2.7.6_4 gnomehier-3.0 pciids-20140502 expat-2.1.0 damageproto-1.2.1 dri2proto-2.8 fixesproto-5.0 inputproto-2.3 kbproto-1.0.6 libSM-1.2.2_2,1 libICE-1.0.8_2,1 libXau-1.0.8_2 libXdmcp-1.1.1_2 randrproto-1.4.0 renderproto-0.11.1 xextproto-7.3.0 xf86vidmodeproto-2.3.1 xproto-7.0.25.
Creating bzip'd tar ball in '/misc/work/ports/misc/ports/devel/libgsf/work/pkg/libgsf-1.14.28_1.tbz'
===>  Installing for libgsf-1.14.28_1
===>  Checking if devel/libgsf already installed
pkg_add: could not find package broken_contents !
*** Error code 1

Stop in /misc/ports/devel/libgsf.

... same error happened with gtk 2.0 port (reported to mailing list: http://www.mail-archive.com/freebsd-ports@freebsd.org/msg58179.html).

Some of other ports were installed/updated however ...

  ===>>> The following actions were performed:
        Upgrade of curl-7.36.0 to curl-7.37.0
        Upgrade of libogg-1.3.1,4 to libogg-1.3.2,4
        Upgrade of flac-1.3.0_1 to flac-1.3.0_2
        Upgrade of libmad-0.15.1b_2 to libmad-0.15.1b_5
        Upgrade of libmodplug-0.8.8.4 to libmodplug-0.8.8.5
        Upgrade of libvorbis-1.3.4,3 to libvorbis-1.3.4_1,3

Is the problem in manifest of newly built package or the installed port?
